title: Iterable Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Iterable** is a cross-channel growth-marketing platform exposing a REST API suite under `https://api.iterable.com/api` (US) and `https://api.eu.iterable.com/api` (EU). This connector — `IterableConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "iterable"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from an Iterable workspace:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Users | `/users` | Get/upsert/bulk-update profiles, export by list, token registration, identifier merge, GDPR delete |
| Events | `/events` | Track single + bulk custom events for the analytics stream |
| Campaigns | `/campaigns` | List + get + create blast/triggered/recurring campaigns |
| Templates | `/templates` | List + get email/push/SMS/in-app message templates |
| Channels | `/channels` | List Iterable messaging channels (email/SMS/push/in-app/web push) |
| Lists | `/lists` | List + create + delete static lists; subscribe / unsubscribe; export users |
| Catalogs | `/catalogs` | List catalogs, list items, upsert single + bulk items |
| Workflows | `/workflows` | Trigger a workflow for a user with optional dataFields |
| Send (Email) | `/email/target` | Trigger a one-off transactional email |
| Send (SMS) | `/sms/target` | Trigger a one-off transactional SMS |
| Send (Push) | `/push/target` | Trigger a transactional mobile push |
| In-App | `/inApp/target` + `/inApp/getMessages` | Trigger and retrieve in-app messages |

The connector normalises **templates + lists** into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + Retry-After awareness (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::IterableHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Server-Side API Key

1. Sign in to [Iterable](https://app.iterable.com/) (or `https://app.eu.iterable.com/` for EU workspaces).
2. Open **Integrations → API Keys**.
3. Click **New API Key** and choose **Server-Side** as the key type. Mobile / JWT keys will be rejected — this connector calls server-only endpoints (`/users/update`, `/events/track`, `/email/target`, …).
4. Name it (e.g. `shielva-iterable-prod`) and grant scopes for Users, Events, Lists, Templates, Catalogs, Workflows, Channels, Campaigns, Send.
5. Copy the key value — Iterable shows it only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Iterable**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | the key from step 5 |
| `region` (string, optional) | `us` (default) or `eu` |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | leave blank unless Iterable assigned you a custom endpoint |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | informational soft cap, default 100 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present, persists non-secret config, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`. It does NOT call any Iterable endpoint at install time.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which performs `GET /lists` as a lightweight probe. Outcomes:

| HTTP | Result |
|---|---|
| 2xx | `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)` |
| 401 | `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` — key revoked / wrong workspace |
| 403 | `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, FAILED)` — key lacks scope |
| 429 | `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)` + structured-log warning |
| network | `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)` — transport error |

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Iterable REST API uses **server-to-server API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent in the **`Api-Key`** header on every request.
- `Authorization: Bearer <key>` is explicitly **rejected** by Iterable — do not use it.
- The base URL is derived from `region` (`us` → `https://api.iterable.com/api`, `eu` → `https://api.eu.iterable.com/api`), with `base_url` taking precedence when set.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`. Persists `region`, `base_url`, `rate_limit_per_min` via `save_config`. Does **not** call any Iterable endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="api_key")` for ABI compat. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /lists` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Api-Key: <api_key>
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json
```

For the user-export endpoint (`/lists/getUsers`) the client switches `Accept: text/plain` and parses newline-delimited emails via `helpers.utils.parse_user_export`.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `Api-Key` header value |
| `region` | string | no | `self.region` (default `"us"`) | Picks the base URL when `base_url` is blank |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` | Explicit base URL override |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Informational soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE = "iterable"
CONNECTOR_NAME = "Iterable"
AUTH_TYPE      = "api_key"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_ITERABLE_BASE_URL    = "https://api.iterable.com/api"
_ITERABLE_EU_BASE_URL = "https://api.eu.iterable.com/api"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `IterableConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::IterableHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; persist `region`, `base_url`, `rate_limit_per_min`. No API call.

### `async authorize(auth_code="", state="") -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="api_key")`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Calls `GET /lists`; classifies failures via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates `list_templates(template_type="Triggered")` + `list_lists()`, normalises each (`normalize_template` / `normalize_list_as_document`), calls `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_found / _synced / _failed`.

children.title: Users
children.content:
### `async get_user(*, email=None, user_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /users/getByEmail?email=...` OR `GET /users/byUserId/{user_id}`. Returns the flattened user dict (unwraps `{"user": {...}}`).

### `async update_user(email=None, user_id=None, data_fields=None, merge_nested_objects=True) -> Dict`
`POST /users/update` body: `{email|userId, dataFields, mergeNestedObjects}`.

### `async bulk_update_users(users) -> Dict`
`POST /users/bulkUpdate` body: `{users: [...]}` — up to 1 000 users.

### `async list_users(list_id) -> List[str]`
`GET /lists/getUsers?listId={N}` — newline-delimited email export.

### `async register_browser_token(email|user_id, browser_token) -> Dict`
`POST /users/registerBrowserToken`.

### `async update_email(current_email, new_email) -> Dict`
`POST /users/updateEmail` — migrate identifier without losing history.

### `async delete_user(email=None, user_id=None) -> Dict`
`DELETE /users/{email}` or `DELETE /users/byUserId/{userId}` — GDPR delete.

children.title: Events
children.content:
### `async track_event(email, event_name, data_fields=None, campaign_id=None, template_id=None, user_id=None, event_id=None, created_at=None) -> Dict`
`POST /events/track`. Body built by `helpers.utils.build_event_payload`.

### `async bulk_track_events(events) -> Dict`
`POST /events/trackBulk` body: `{events: [...]}`. Non-empty list enforced.

children.title: Campaigns
children.content:
### `async list_campaigns() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /campaigns` — unwraps `{"campaigns": [...]}`.

### `async get_campaign(campaign_id) -> Dict`
`GET /campaigns/{id}` — falls back to `GET /campaigns/metrics?campaignId=` on 404 (legacy workspaces).

### `async create_triggered_campaign(name, list_ids, template_id, send_at=None, suppression_list_ids=None, data_fields=None) -> Dict`
`POST /campaigns/create`.

children.title: Templates + Channels
children.content:
### `async list_templates(template_type="Triggered", message_medium="Email") -> Dict`
`GET /templates?templateType=&messageMedium=`.

### `async get_template(template_id) -> Dict`
`GET /templates/{id}`.

### `async list_channels() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /channels` — unwraps `{"channels": [...]}`.

children.title: Lists
children.content:
### `async list_lists() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /lists` — unwraps `{"lists": [...]}`.

### `async create_list(name) -> Dict`
`POST /lists` body: `{name}`. Returns `{"listId": N}`.

### `async delete_list(list_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /lists/{listId}`.

### `async subscribe_to_list(list_id, subscribers) -> Dict`
`POST /lists/subscribe`. Each subscriber must carry `email` or `userId` (validated via `normalize_subscribers`).

### `async unsubscribe_from_list(list_id, subscribers, campaign_id=None, channel_unsubscribe=False) -> Dict`
`POST /lists/unsubscribe`.

children.title: Catalogs
children.content:
### `async list_catalogs() -> List[str]`
`GET /catalogs` — handles both `{params: {catalogNames: [...]}}` (legacy) and `{catalogs: [...]}` (modern) shapes via `normalize_catalogs`.

### `async list_catalog_items(catalog_name, page=1, page_size=100) -> Dict`
`GET /catalogs/{name}/items`.

### `async upsert_catalog_item(catalog_name, item_id, value) -> Dict`
`PUT /catalogs/{name}/items/{itemId}` body: `{value}`.

### `async bulk_upsert_catalog_items(catalog_name, documents) -> Dict`
`POST /catalogs/{name}/items` body: `{documents: {...}}`.

children.title: Workflows + Send APIs
children.content:
### `async trigger_workflow(workflow_id, email=None, user_id=None, list_id=None, data_fields=None) -> Dict`
`POST /workflows/triggerWorkflow`. Requires at least one target identifier.

### `async send_email(campaign_id, recipient_email, data_fields=None, send_at=None, allow_repeat_marketing_sends=None, metadata=None) -> Dict`
`POST /email/target`. The target campaign must be a Triggered (transactional) campaign.

### `async send_sms(campaign_id, recipient_email, data_fields=None) -> Dict`
`POST /sms/target`.

### `async send_push(campaign_id, recipient_email=None, recipient_user_id=None, data_fields=None) -> Dict`
`POST /push/target`. Requires at least one recipient identifier.

### `async get_in_app_messages(email=None, user_id=None, count=100, platform="All", sdk_version=None) -> Dict`
`GET /inApp/getMessages`.

### `async send_in_app(campaign_id, recipient_email=None, recipient_user_id=None, data_fields=None) -> Dict`
`POST /inApp/target`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
IterableError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── IterableAuthError                   # 401 / 403
├── IterableBadRequestError             # 400
├── IterableNotFoundError               # 404
├── IterableConflictError               # 409
├── IterableRateLimitError              # 429 — retry_after_s
├── IterableServerError                 # 5xx
└── IterableNetworkError                # transport / timeout
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
IterableNotFound = IterableNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status / condition | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately (typed exception) |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` (capped at 30 s); otherwise `0.5 * 2 ** attempt + jitter`; retry up to 3 |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.HTTPError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `IterableNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches and maps:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, FAILED)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)            (logged warning)
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
other  → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-enter the Server-Side key in ACP. Iterable shows the key only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, FAILED)` at health_check | 403 from Iterable — key lacks the required project scope | In Iterable → Integrations → API Keys, edit the key and grant Users / Events / Lists / Templates scopes for the surfaces you use. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — key revoked, wrong workspace, or pointed at the wrong region | Verify `region` matches the workspace (US vs EU). Rotate the key if needed. |
| `iterable.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Iterable per-minute rate cap) | The client honours `Retry-After` and backs off. Lower throughput, batch via `bulk_track_events` / `bulk_update_users`, or shard by `workflow_id`. |
| 404 from `get_campaign(id)` then 200 from `/campaigns/metrics` | Legacy workspace exposes campaign detail under `/campaigns/metrics` | No action — the connector's automatic fallback handles this. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'IterableNotFound'` | Older import path used | Already fixed — `IterableNotFound = IterableNotFoundError` alias preserved in `exceptions.py`. |
| `Authorization: Bearer ...` ends up in the request | Wrong header in a custom override | Never set `Authorization`. Iterable expects `Api-Key: <key>` and rejects bearer-style headers with 401. |
| `list_users` returns an empty list for a known list | `text/plain` parsing failure or list emptied recently | Verify the listId; manually `curl -H "Api-Key: $KEY" $BASE/lists/getUsers?listId=N`. The helper handles both newline-delimited text and `{emails: [...]}` JSON. |
